title: hieracium erioleion subsp. gnaphalodes
intro: Hieracium erioleion subsp. gnaphalodes, commonly known as Hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Hawkweed include its ability to reduce swelling, promote healing, and alleviate symptoms of eczema and other skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Hawkweed is often made into a topical cream or salve, which can be applied directly to the affected area to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
